name: Digital LinkWorks
lifecycle_note: Discontinued after DEC was acquired by Compaq (1998); product not carried forward under HP.

name: Lotus Notes
lifecycle_note: Acquired by IBM 1995; evolved through IBM Lotus Notes to HCL Notes after HCL Technologies acquired the product line from IBM in 2019.

name: Microsoft Exchange
lifecycle_note: Still actively developed and widely deployed as of 2026; available as Exchange Server and Exchange Online (Microsoft 365).

name: OLE (Object Linking and Embedding)
lifecycle_note: OLE evolved into COM/DCOM and ActiveX; concepts persist in modern Windows development and COM automation.

name: Distributed Object Technology / CORBA
lifecycle_note: Superseded by web services (SOAP/REST), SOA, and microservices architectures; OMG CORBA standard technically maintained but rarely used in new development.

name: Digital UNIX (Tru64)
lifecycle_note: Renamed Tru64 UNIX under Compaq; end-of-life support ended by HP around 2012.

name: HP-UX
lifecycle_note: HP-UX still supported as of 2026 for Integrity servers; Hewlett Packard Enterprise announced end of hardware support for Itanium in 2025.

name: OpenVMS
lifecycle_note: VMS Software Inc. (VSI) acquired OpenVMS from HP in 2014 and continues active development as of 2026.

name: IBM AIX
lifecycle_note: IBM AIX remains actively supported as of 2026 for IBM Power Systems.

name: Windows NT
lifecycle_note: Windows NT 3.5 end of life 2001; NT kernel architecture evolved through Windows 2000, XP, and into all modern Windows versions.

name: Windows 3.1 / Windows for Workgroups
lifecycle_note: End of life December 2001; superseded by Windows 95, 98, and later Windows XP.

name: Digital All-in-1
lifecycle_note: DEC's All-in-1 office automation suite discontinued after DEC acquisition by Compaq. No modern successor.

name: Oracle Database
lifecycle_note: Oracle Database still actively developed and deployed as of 2026 (Oracle 23c).

name: Microsoft SQL Server
lifecycle_note: Microsoft SQL Server still actively developed and deployed as of 2026.

name: RPC (Remote Procedure Call)
lifecycle_note: RPC concept evolved into web services, gRPC, and REST APIs; DCE RPC itself largely obsolete in new development.

name: OS/2 Presentation Manager
lifecycle_note: IBM discontinued mainstream OS/2 support in 2006; ArcaOS (Arca Noae) provides continued support for legacy deployments.

name: Macintosh OS
lifecycle_note: Classic Mac OS superseded by Mac OS X (now macOS) in 2001; macOS Sequoia current as of 2026.

name: Motif (X Window GUI)
lifecycle_note: Motif largely obsolete; superseded by modern GUI toolkits such as GTK and Qt on Linux/Unix.

name: Document Management & Workflow (BPM)
lifecycle_note: Document management and workflow concepts evolved into Business Process Management (BPM), ECM, and low-code automation platforms.
successor: BPM platforms, Microsoft Power Automate, ServiceNow
